hotspots.title: Israel intercepts Gaza aid flotilla, detains Italians
hotspots.blurb: Italy's government, led by Giorgia Meloni, has condemned Israel's interception of a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters, demanding the immediate release of detained Italian activists. The incident has sparked widespread outrage and diplomatic tensions, with multiple Italian news outlets covering the government's response and public protests.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral outrage over humanitarian aid being blocked, the diplomatic strain between Italy and Israel, and the symbolic significance of the flotilla as a protest against Gaza's blockade.

hotspots.title: Trump administration’s Iran war costs and strategies
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration's military actions against Iran have cost $25 billion, with ongoing debates over strategies like blockades and hypersonic missile strikes. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced tough questions from lawmakers about the war's financial and strategic implications, while Trump considers reducing U.S. troops in Germany.
hotspots.affect: This story dominates due to the high-stakes geopolitical tensions, the financial burden of the conflict, and the polarized debate over U.S. military strategy and alliances.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain's conservative People's Party (PP) and far-right Vox have formed regional government pacts in Aragón and Extremadura, implementing Vox's 'national priority' policies. These agreements prioritize Spaniards in public services, sparking backlash from opposition parties and unions over perceived discrimination and erosion of labor rights.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the charged debate over immigration, national identity, and the rise of far-right policies in mainstream governance, with significant implications for Spain's political landscape.

hotspots.title: Magyar’s EU funds deal and Sovereignty Office abolition
hotspots.blurb: Hungary's new Prime Minister Péter Magyar has secured a preliminary deal with the EU to unlock €17 billion in frozen funds, contingent on abolishing the controversial Sovereignty Protection Office. The move signals a shift from Viktor Orbán's nationalist policies and has sparked intense debate over Hungary's future direction and EU relations.
hotspots.affect: This story is highly charged due to the high financial stakes, the symbolic break from Orbán's authoritarian legacy, and the polarized debate over Hungary's sovereignty versus EU integration.

hotspots.title: PiS internal feud over Łukasz Mejza’s expulsion
hotspots.blurb: Poland's ruling Law and Justice (PiS) party expelled controversial MP Łukasz Mejza from its parliamentary club after years of scandals, including traffic violations and plans to participate in freak fight events. The move highlights deepening internal divisions within PiS, with factions led by Jarosław Kaczyński and Mateusz Morawiecki vying for control ahead of the 2027 elections.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to the spectacle of internal party strife, the moral outrage over Mejza's scandals, and the high-stakes power struggle within Poland's dominant political party.
